content.problemStatement:
There is a **directed** acyclic graph with **N** vertices. The vertices are numbered from **1** to **N**.


	For every **1 ≤ i ≤ N-1**, there is an edge from vertex **i** to vertex **i+1**, which has weight **ai**.


	For every **1 ≤ i ≤ N-2**, there is an edge from vertex **i** to vertex **i+2**, which has weight **bi**.


	For every **1 ≤ i ≤ N-3**, there is an edge from vertex **i** to vertex **i+3**, which has weight **ci**.


	There are no other edges.


	For each pair of vertices **s**, **t** where **s < t**, let **d(s, t)** denote the length of the shortest path from **s** to **t**. Your task is to compute the sum of **d(s, t)** for every **1 ≤ s < t ≤ N.**
constraints: 1 ≤ sum of N over all test cases ≤ 3*10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T denoting the number of test cases. Each test case consists of four lines. The first line contains an integer N. The second line contains N-1 integers a1, a2, ..., aN-1. The third line contains N-2 integers b1, b2, ..., bN-2. The fourth line contains N-3 integers c1, c2, ..., cN-3.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing an integer, denoting the sum of d(s, t) for every 1 ≤ s < t ≤ N.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the distance between each pair of vertices is 1, so the sum of the distances is 6. For the second test case, the distances are calculated based on the given edge weights.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming to calculate the shortest distance between each pair of vertices. We start by initializing the distance array with the given edge weights. Then, we iterate over each vertex and update the distance array based on the shortest path from the current vertex to all other vertices. Finally, we calculate the sum of the shortest distances between all pairs of vertices.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*N^2) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the number of vertices. The time complexity is dominated by the nested loops used to calculate the shortest distances between all pairs of vertices.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — where N is the number of vertices. The space complexity is dominated by the distance array used to store the shortest distances between each pair of vertices.
